attributes.description: Alerts when a store reports any intentional write stalls due to disk backpressure (`storage_write_stalls > 0`). Write stalls are a direct user impact: SQL writes will be latent or failing while they persist.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] Storage write stalls detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB storage write stalls detected

### What fired
The gauge `storage_write_stalls` on at least one store exceeded 0 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
Write stalls are intentional back-pressure by the storage engine to prevent the LSM from collapsing. While they are preferable to an unavailable store, they cause direct user-visible latency spikes for any workload writing through that store.

### Triage
1. On the Storage dashboard, correlate stalls with `admission_io_overload`, `storage_l0_sublevels`, `storage_l0_num_files`, and `rocksdb_read_amplification`.
2. Check disk queue (`sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress`) and host I/O.
3. Check CPU — compaction starvation can cause stalls.

### Remediation
- Increase provisioned IOPS / upgrade storage.
- Shed or throttle the write workload temporarily.
- Add nodes to spread the write load.

### Tuning
- This rule fires on any stall. For noisy environments, require `> N` over a longer window.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND storage_write_stalls IS NOT NULL
  AND storage_write_stalls > 0
// Store-level metric: intentional write stalls due to disk backpressure
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NOT NULL
| STATS stalls = MAX(storage_write_stalls) BY attributes.node_id, attributes.store, host.name
| SORT stalls DESC
